ViewState.dealerHand: { cards: Card[], total: number|null, isBust: boolean }
ViewState.playerHands: { cards: Card[], total: number, bet: number, isActive: boolean, isBust: boolean, isBlackjack: boolean, outcome: string|null }[]
ViewState.currentBetInput: number — value to prefill bet input during betting phase
ViewState.message: string — outcome or status message to display
ViewState.canReset: boolean — true when bankroll exhausted, surfaces Reset button

render.returns: void — idempotently repaints rootEl to reflect state

getActionButtonId.returns: string — stable DOM id for the action button, one of 'btn-hit','btn-stand','btn-double','btn-split'

notes: UIRender exposes a single render(state, rootEl) entry point. The controller calls render after every state transition. UIRender does not bind events; the controller queries elements by the stable IDs above and attaches listeners. Action buttons that should be disabled (not in legalActions) get the 'disabled' attribute set by render. The Reset button is hidden unless state.canReset is true. Mobile responsiveness is achieved via CSS media queries in styles.css; no JS-side responsive logic.
